g.text: "Fresh Batteries and a Ball of String"

Head down the midway to the carousel.
Step back to the midway crossroads and c...
Slip into the hall of mirrors.
Take the steps down under the boardwalk.
Follow the lamplight to the caretaker's ...
Go straight to the chained carousel.
Slide behind the counter to the ledger s...
Ease open the little back office.
Open the newest ledger at the paper book...
Take what you've learned back to the cro...
Go look at the carousel itself with new ...
Carry the clue back to the crossroads.
If the measures stay with the herd, go t...
Hold your torch close to the photograph.
Sink into the big chair and sort through...
Head back to the crossroads to search on...
Go straight to the lead horse and her ca...
Take the left passage of tall mirrors.
Take the right passage of bent mirrors.
Call a brave hello into the dark.
Follow the bend to the mirrored rotunda.
Slip back out to the midway crossroads.
Steady yourself and keep on toward the r...
Kneel and lift the loose plank.
Go on to the mirrored rotunda.
Head back out to the crossroads.
Track the flicker moving up high.
Hurry along the painted arrows the way y...
Go ask the sea mare her secret yourself.
Climb down and go to that saddle.
Climb down and go back to the crossroads...
Lift the dust sheet from the horse-sized...
Carry the little rose chisel to the caro...
Close the chest and head back to the cro...
Cover the colt gently and go back out.
O is for Ondine. Go find the lead horse.
Dig through the crate of offcuts.
Go back to the crossroads to think.
Go find the finished hiding place on the...
Pick your way between the pilings.
Climb up to the dry catwalk.
Wade on to the carousel's center footing...
Climb the rusty ladder up to the catwalk...
Explore out toward the dark arches.
Shine your torch on the big center beam.
Follow the catwalk toward the pier head.
Climb back up and look at the carousel w...
Head back up to the crossroads.
Keep still and watch the figure.
Go to the carousel he cares for so much.
Follow the bobbing lantern toward the ca...
Thank him and head for the crossroads.
Peep through the curtain gap first.
Slip away to the crossroads and think.
Sit down at his little table.
Ask which horse was Gran's favorite.
Ask about the shims under the pier.
Ask for every story about Gran, all of t...
So where would Edith hide her measures? ...
Say goodnight and go back to the crossro...
Go to Ondine together, lantern and all.
Step out to the crossroads to steady you...
Stop on the bottom step and just listen.
Leave the organ to its humming and go ba...
Lift the crest panel with the carved ros...
Freeze and count slowly to fifty.
Bolt down the stairs and away.
Take the hint and go to Ondine.
Pocket the program and return to the cro...
Take the tracing straight to the lead ho...
Carry the waltz roll home to learn its s...
Put the roll back carefully and go down ...
Go down to that winking seam.
Go down and back to the crossroads.
Duck under the chain and climb up to Ond...
Circle the platform and read the notice ...
Step back to the crossroads first.
Press the smooth center of the carved ro...
Try to pry the seam open with the key ri...
Press it with your thumb, the way Gran m...
Whisper sorry to Ondine and save it for ...
Unroll the drawings right there by torch...
Knock at the caretaker's caravan with th...
Run home and wake your family.
Wait for morning and carry it to the tow...
Call the town to a meeting at the fair i...
Go with him to the town surveyor at open...
Watch the pledge board fill to the botto...
Shake on it and start that same week.
Start a Pennies for Ondine drive at scho...
Sign the whole family up as repair crew.
Paint the thermometer to the top.
Present the drawings at the public counc...
Show the story to the local paper first.
Pin the front page to the ticket booth.
